title: ProtocolLab Public Report Contract Architecture
purpose: Describe the storage-neutral public report contract boundary.
design_summary: Public report schemas define the semantic payload. Implementations produce and store report bundles outside this public repository while preserving public claim and provenance rules.
data_and_state_considerations: Report consumers should treat the schema-versioned evidence report as the semantic source. Derived indexes and storage-specific locations are consumer or implementation state.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Diagnostic reports remain diagnostic even when they are publicly visible.
Unsupported and unavailable outcomes remain visible to readers.
QUIC transport and HTTP/3 result lanes remain distinct.
alternatives_considered: Defining a storage-specific upload path in the public contract was rejected because storage is an implementation concern.
Using derived browse indexes as the semantic source was rejected because derived state can omit evidence details.
risks: Report consumers may accidentally upgrade claims unless they preserve claim-level fields.
Storage-specific implementations may need adapter documentation outside this public repository.
